rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative bromolactonization of 4-aryl-pent-4-enoic acids with NaBr to give bromomethyl-substituted gamma-butyrolactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide to bromine radical (and subsequently to a bromocation equivalent), which initiates alkene functionalization; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: Bromine radical (Br•) generated by anodic oxidation of Br-
Carbon-centered radical I from Br• addition to alkene of 4-phenylpent-4-enoic acid
Benzylic carbocation II from anodic oxidation of radical I

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br•, which adds to the terminal alkene of the pentenoic acid to give a benzylic C-radical (I); a second anodic SET oxidizes I to a benzylic cation (II), which is trapped intramolecularly by the carboxylate to release H+ and deliver the bromomethyl gamma-lactone, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reduced yield of product, consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reduced yield of product, consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Molecular Br2 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product 3a formed, indicating that free Br2 is not the active brominating intermediate

probe_reagents.name: No-electricity control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without applied current, confirming electrochemical initiation

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and NaBr
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks observed at 1.76 V (1a) and 0.63 V (NaBr), indicating Br- is oxidized preferentially at the anode

notes: Authors explicitly rule out molecular Br2 as the active intermediate based on the Br2-only control giving trace product. Mechanism is a radical-polar crossover (Br• addition then anodic oxidation to cation, then carboxylate cyclization), supported by radical-trap suppression and CV data but no KIE or DFT.
